content.problemStatement:
Shlok and Sachin are good friends. Shlok wanted to test Sachin, so he wrote down a string SSS with length NNN and one character XXX. He wants Sachin to find the number of different substrings of SSS which contain the character XXX at least once. Sachin is busy with his girlfriend, so he needs you to find the answer.

Two substrings of SSS are considered different if their positions in SSS are different.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase English letters
XXX is a lowercase English letter
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (30 points):** the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10310^3103
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains a string SSS with length NNN, followed by a space and a character XXX.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of substrings of SSS that contain XXX.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The string "abb" has six substrings: "a", "b", "b", "ab", "bb", "abb". The substrings that contain 'b' are "b", "b", "ab", "bb", "abb".
